initial_diagnostic_question.question: Does this ECG suggest the presence of first degree AV block?

criterion_selection.question: To accurately diagnose first degree AV block, which of the following diagnostic criteria should be evaluated?
options: Presence of retrograde P waves following QRS complexes
Presence of an RSR' pattern in leads V1 and V2
Complete dissociation between P waves and QRS complexes

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, looking at the overall rhythm, is every P wave consistently followed by a QRS complex on this ECG?

decision.question: Based on the finding identified above, does this patient have first degree AV block?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
decision.answer: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis

criterion_selection.question: In addition to the finding you just identified, which other diagnostic criterion should be evaluated to diagnose first degree AV block?
options: Alternating duration of the PR interval
Beat-to-beat variability in the PR interval
Presence of an RSR' pattern in leads V1 and V2

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, is the PR interval prolonged on this ECG? Note that a PR interval is considered to be prolonged if it is greater than 200 milliseconds.

grounding.question: In which of the following segments can you observe a P wave that demonstrates a non-prolonged PR interval? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG signal, provided in seconds.
For the selected segment, which range does the measured PR interval fall into?

decision.question: Based on all the findings identified so far, does this patient have first degree AV block?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
